svg.text: k sorted lists, total N elements. Goal: produce a single sorted output
or answer a query (k-th smallest, smallest range covering all).
Heap-based merge: O(N log k) time, O(k) heap.
Push the head of every list with a back-pointer (list-id, index).
Pop the smallest, emit, push the next item of that list.
Pairwise merge is O(N k) — strictly worse for k > 2.
• At each step you need the global minimum of k frontiers — heap gives O(log k).
• The heap size is bounded by k regardless of N.
• Back-pointers let you advance only the list that contributed.
• Generalises: matrix as rows, fraction generators, range-cover problems.
External-sort tape merge uses the same structure on disk-resident lists.
Anywhere you have k sorted streams: heap of frontiers.
